# -*- sh -*-
# See http://rigaux.org/language-study/diagram.html for the format of this file
#claude: auto-generated by Claude Code (inspired from my other lineage.txt)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Profiler lineage
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

#src: wikipedia articles on different programs

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pre-Historical
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

# profiling concept goes back to IBM System/360 timer interrupts (1960s)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Historical (UNIX)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*prof
  1975
  # UNIX V6, hybrid: pc sampling (profil(2)) + call counts (cc -p / mcount)

*gprof
  1982
  prof
  SY:2
  # Susan Graham, UC Berkeley, BSD 4.2
  # added call graph profiling to prof

*GNU gprof
  1988
  gprof
  SY:2
  # GNU binutils port of BSD gprof for GCC toolchain
  # same -pg / mcount mechanism, added flat profile sorting options
  # the version most Linux users invoke as "gprof" today

sprof
  1998
  GNU gprof
  # GNU libc shared-library profiler
  # profiles calls through PLT stubs, no recompilation needed

*pixie
  1989
  prof
  SY:6
  # MIPS/SGI, basic block counting via binary instrumentation
  # very accurate counts, influenced later tools

*ATOM
  1994
  pixie
  # DEC, binary analysis/instrumentation framework
  # influenced Pin, Valgrind

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plan 9
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*prof (Plan 9)
  1993
  prof
  SY:1
  # Plan 9 profiler, requires loader instrumentation (2l -p)

*tprof
  1993
  SY:1
  # tick-based sampling profiler, reads counters from /proc

*kprof
  1993
  tprof
  SY:1
  # kernel profiler, accumulates ticks per kernel PC bucket
  # reads /dev/kpctl and /dev/kprof; part of Plan 9 profiler suite

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Open source
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*gcov
  1996
  pixie
  SY:2
  # GCC coverage tool, source-level statement counts
  # compile with -fprofile-arcs -ftest-coverage
  # strictly a coverage tool (line/branch hit counts), not a time profiler

*OProfile
  2002
  prof
  SY:2
  # John Levon, system-wide statistical profiler for Linux
  # uses hardware performance counters

*perf
  2009
  OProfile
  SY:2
  # Linux kernel subsystem (perf_events), replaced OProfile
  # hardware counters, tracepoints, kprobes

*Valgrind callgrind
  2002
  ATOM
  SY:2
  # Josef Weidendorfer, cache and call-graph profiler
  # part of Valgrind, used with KCachegrind visualizer

*gperftools
  2005
  prof
  SY:2
  # Google, CPU profiler + tcmalloc + heap profiler
  # sampling-based, ships pprof Python script for visualization

*FlameGraph
  2011
  # Brendan Gregg, Netflix
  # visualization of profiled stack traces, hugely influential

*pprof (gperftools)
  2005
  gperftools
  # original pprof: Python script bundled with gperftools
  # reads gperftools CPU profiles, renders call graphs

*pprof (Go)
  2012
  pprof (gperftools)
  # Go runtime profiling tool, same name/concept, rewritten in Go
  # standard Go toolchain; proto-based profile format now cross-language

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tracing / Dynamic instrumentation
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*DTrace
  2005
  SY:6
  # Bryan Cantrill, Sun/Solaris
  # dynamic tracing framework, safe kernel probes
  # D language for probe scripts

*SystemTap
  2005
  DTrace
  SY:2
  # Red Hat, Linux answer to DTrace
  # translates scripts to kernel modules

*ftrace
  2008
  SY:2
  # Steven Rostedt, Linux kernel function tracer
  # trace-cmd frontend, /sys/kernel/tracing

*LTTng
  2006
  SY:2
  # Mathieu Desnoyers, Linux Trace Toolkit next generation
  # low-overhead kernel and userspace tracing

*BPF/bpftrace
  2016
  DTrace,perf
  SY:2
  # Brendan Gregg et al, DTrace-like for Linux via eBPF
  # bpftrace (2018) provides high-level tracing language

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Industry
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*Rational Quantify
  1995
  gprof
  SY:6
  # Rational Software (later IBM), dominant commercial call-graph profiler
  # call-count instrumentation at link time, no source changes needed
  # widely used in enterprise Unix/Windows through the 1990s-2000s

*Intel VTune
  1998
  SY:6
  # hardware counter-based profiler for x86
  # gold standard for CPU microarchitecture analysis

*Visual Studio Profiler
  2005
  SY:6
  # Microsoft, integrated sampling and instrumentation profiler

*Instruments
  2007
  DTrace
  SY:6
  # Apple, Xcode profiling tool, built on DTrace
  # Time Profiler, Allocations, Leaks

*AMD uProf
  2017
  Intel VTune
  SY:6
  # AMD's profiler for Zen architecture

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Academic / Research
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*DCPI
  1997
  # Jeff Dean et al, DEC Western Research Lab, SOSP 1997
  # "Where Have All the Cycles Gone?", always-on continuous profiling
  # influenced Google-Wide Profiling and cloud profiling industry

*Google-Wide Profiling
  2010
  DCPI
  SY:6
  # continuous fleet-wide profiling at Google
  # paper at OSDI 2010, influenced cloud profiling products

*Pin
  2004
  ATOM
  SY:2
  # Intel, dynamic binary instrumentation framework
  # JIT-based, language-neutral; foundation for many analysis tools (Pintools)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Recent
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*Tracy
  2017
  # real-time frame profiler for game development
  # C++, nanosecond resolution, very popular in gamedev

*Pyroscope
  2021
  pprof (Go)
  # continuous profiling platform, open source
  # acquired by Grafana Labs

*Parca
  2022
  Google-Wide Profiling,pprof (Go)
  # continuous profiling for cloud-native, eBPF-based
  # open source alternative to Google-Wide Profiling

samply
  2022
  perf
  # Markus Stange, sampling profiler for Linux/macOS
  # Firefox Profiler UI, Rust
